title: Ranunculastrum auricomum
taxonomy: Ranunculastrum auricomum is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. It belongs to the genus Ranunculastrum. The plant is classified in the kingdom Plantae, the phylum Magnoliophyta, the class Magnoliopsida, the order Ranunculales, and the family Ranunculaceae. The Ranunculastrum genus is closely related to the genus Trollius and the plant is often found in moist, grassy areas. Its classification is part of the larger plant taxonomy system that groups plants based on their shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Ranunculastrum auricomum, also known as Bear's Foot or Goldilocks, is a perennial plant species in the family Ranunculaceae, characterized by its golden-yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. Bioactive constituents of Ranunculastrum auricomum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ranunculastrum auricomum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support digestive health and skin well-being.
